differentials.name: Café-au-lait spots =  tan / light brown pigmented often ovoid cutaneous macules + patches with irregular borders ≥ 6 in number > 5 mm in greatest diameter prior to puberty > 15 mm in postpubertal individuals
differentials.raw: Café-au-lait spots =  tan / light brown pigmented often ovoid cutaneous macules + patches with irregular borders ≥ 6 in number > 5 mm in greatest diameter prior to puberty > 15 mm in postpubertal individuals

differentials.name: Intertriginous freckling (= Crowe sign) =  pigmented cutaneous macules < 5 mm in size

differentials.name: Dermal (cutaneous) neurofibroma Histo:  benign mixture of Schwann cells + fibroblasts + perineural cells + mast cells Age:  begins to appear around early childhood / puberty subsequent to detection of café-au-lait spots (a)  localized = fibroma molluscum = string of pearls along peripheral nerve

context: DDx:   "coast-of-Maine" spots of McCune-Albright syndrome (with more jagged borders) Age:  usually present at birth, increase in number over first 1-2 years of life ◊  One of the earliest manifestations of NF1 Histo:  increased melanin pigment in basal epidermal layer DDx:  tuberous sclerosis, fibrous dysplasia Extent:   often parallels disease severity Age:  3-5 years Location:  intertriginous skin of axilla (in 66%), groin, submammary fold, neck Cx:   NO malignant degeneration! (b)  plexiform neurofibroma = multilobulated tortuous entanglement / interdigitating network of tumor along a nerve
